page: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, configured with 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with multithreading. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 system memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, featuring 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 450 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the g6e.2xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. This includes prompt processing and text generation for small and medium models, as well as prompt processing for large models. Single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth for read operations, and general processing tasks like software compilation and database operations fall within the average range. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in large-block memory write bandwidth, which registers in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g6e.2xlarge offers a specialized balance of dedicated AMD EPYC compute, local NVMe storage, and high-capacity GPU resources. The inclusion of 45 GB of VRAM on the NVIDIA L40S GPU makes this instance highly resource-dense for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads without requiring the overhead of multi-GPU configurations. Appropriate workloads for this instance include LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and graphics-heavy applications that benefit from local NVMe SSD caching and dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a dedicated NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in large language model inference, including prompt processing and text generation for 135M and 7B models. Single-core CPU performance is strong, though multi-core CPU and read memory bandwidth benchmarks are average, and large-block memory write bandwidth is weak. This hardware profile provides qualitative resource density for GPU-accelerated tasks. The g6e.2xlarge is suited for LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and workloads requiring dedicated GPU acceleration paired with local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU, 64 GB RAM, a 45 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge features an AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, a 45 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC compute, local NVMe storage, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU for intensive inference workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB DDR4 RAM
NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
450 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark results for LLM inference and prompt processing
Strong single-core CPU performance with average multi-core metrics
Well-suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and image processing
